Hey JB here time for your weekly intensive It's gonna be a great one and a big week here very busy to the great corporate training down in San Diego spent the rest of the week working on the Certification program, which is just wow it's just going so well It keeps giving me new ideas that to help you guys become more effective because you know as I Forced myself to come up with this like just perfect program here and it's been done Just you know for a while I just keep trying to improve it and prove it before I really release it fully You're gonna be blown away anyway now that being said One of the things that that I did discover this week as I was both my corporate training and through the certification Was one of the important things that really I think I need to highlight even more than I have in the past in terms of in the importance of the structure and flow of the sale and I think that You know what I've said went through this before I've gone through it and explained it It's interesting that I see this again and again and again That when I actually go out and meet the people who are using the straight line system They seem to almost all of them make this like the same major mistake in terms of how they Spread the sale out on the line in terms of you know Whether it's you know trying to stretch things out into too many calls like a three-call system That should be a two-call or a one-call that should be a two-call or better still a one-call That's better off as a hybrid one-call, which I'm gonna go through today along with some other stuff in terms of structure but I think there's a lack of of deeper understanding here of One of the fundamental principles that Guides the entire straight line that really led to its creation and that is the path of least resistance In other words everything that I do When I sell Every word out of my mouth every every move I make every strategic Loop I do a pain. Oh, there's little nips and tucks It's always part of what I consider at least in my own mind at the moment the path of least resistance I try to make it easiest for myself to close the sale and there are certain very obvious things That you should be doing that yet and I explain them you want to go out into the field and I speak to the people who use the straight line a lot of them Honestly was most of them 90% aren't doing it and then when I show them They're like, oh my god, I can't believe you yet So obvious like you're right and then as soon as they implement that their sales literally soar to a whole new level So it's not like it's not this is a small distinction that you know this is something that when you get this right It has a dramatic impact on your closing rate So I want to dig into it much more deeply today and and cover this from a few different angles Person of the phone You know whether it's it leaves a comment from online. And let me draw out the straight line for you open your clothes, right and Remember, right the perfect sale Is represented by the straight line, right? That's everything you say everything you do every word that comes out of your mouth Every document you hand someone Every website you direct them to me Let's say you're closing through a webinar where you have your customer sitting in front of a computer you're sharing your screen with them You do that because it's designed to help you close the sale We don't do things to the sake of doing them with the straight line, right? There's no frills and no extra trimmings. We do everything for a very purposeful Outcome here and that outcome is is to get your prospect to say yes Now depending on what type of call you're on that yes could see could simply be mean setting up An appointment with you for the next call So there's a close on every single call and when I say call That call could be in person or over the phone and In person it could be you going out into the field and knocking on doors or going to preset appointments Or frankly it could be you sitting inside a retail type of business where people are coming into your store whether it's from ads or Incoming from a call call whatever they come to you and you then take them through presentation in that case from the moment They step in your door every bit of your presentation Every bit of the actually the room is designed lay out is really structured as such to create this this path of lease resistance For you to close as many sales as possible and I've been through this with you know for one very examples is window Right vacation metaphor a bunch of years might have a really in-depth About three month long training with them where I really help the redeveloped device a hole into our sales and from who's great to spot what they just want to take it to the next level right and In that case what's happening is have a massive outgoing marketing campaign Whether it's the people handing out coupons and hotels radio TV and you also have newspapers, right? It's just you're thinking correctly there Especially if you have a three call system and you're thinking about collapsing it to a two call I would try everything you could to do that Remember systems that are over two calls are typically because there are multiple decision makers involved and you have to crowd a Bunch of different yeses from the mother people That's the real reason why they're lower sales cycles or this specific to the product that is complex and never Therefore requires the integrated into other parts of the company like I just signed up with a really great company to help We do some customer service stuff right? That contradiction is a contradiction here right? What is it? Well very simply you want to Try to avoid the problem of getting in touch with the same person twice But two call systems are more effective because you've been able to get into rapport It seems that you're really it sends some information The second call is just a warmer call because once someone's spoken to you once that represents You know, that's a notch okay? You're real because now you're back again So there's more trust develop right there's reasons why two calls work better There's sort of chance to become more certain About both your product if you send them out the correct information It really looks makes your product great and also makes you look good in your company You're starting to move up the certainty scale for the three tens before the second call even begins They enter the second call at a higher level of certainty Cold call if you do a cold call and trying to close in that cold call Let's go back to what certainty scale now watch What happens one ten on a cold cold cold?
